name: separator
title: Separator
description: A thin horizontal or vertical rule that separates or groups content. Renders a semantic role="separator" with aria-orientation by default, or an aria-hidden decoration when purely visual. Drawn with a logical border so it stays visible in forced-colors / Windows High Contrast mode.
useWhen: Dividing distinct groups of content or controls where the boundary carries meaning (e.g. between toolbar action groups)
Adding a vertical rule between inline items such as metadata or actions
Reinforcing a section break that assistive technology should announce
avoidWhen: The boundary is already conveyed by a heading, landmark, spacing, or list semantics — mark the rule decorative instead
You need an interactive splitter or resize handle (that is a different, focusable widget)
Purely spacing is required (use margin or padding, not a rule)

keyboard: Not focusable and not interactive — a separator carries no keyboard interaction
accessibility.role: separator (non-decorative) with aria-orientation; aria-hidden with no role when decorative
